response.justification: While Szabo demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals and 20+ years of development experience, he lacks critical hard requirements for this embedded security firmware role. Most critically, he has no native Japanese proficiency (explicitly required), minimal C experience (last used 7 years ago, brief exposure), and zero background in embedded systems, networking, or security domains. His entire career is in web, mobile, and high-level development—fundamentally misaligned with embedded firmware, network switches, and security technologies required.
strengths: 20+ years of software development experience with proven ability to learn diverse technologies across multiple domains
Demonstrated Linux systems familiarity and some C/C++ exposure from National Instruments role
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, code quality) that transfer across any domain
concerns: No native Japanese language proficiency despite being an explicit hard requirement for the position
Minimal C programming experience (only 6 months ~7 years ago); current expertise focused on TypeScript, Node.js, and high-level languages
Zero background in embedded systems, networking protocols, security technologies, or switch/firmware development—entire career is web, mobile, and application-level work

key_factors.factor: C programming expertise for embedded firmware
Embedded networking and security domain experience
